name: Chicken Fiesta
recipeIngredient: 1 ½ teaspoons Chopped fresh thyme or ½ teaspoon dried thyme leaves
1 medium Green bell pepper; coarsely chopped (about 1 cup)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a shallow dish, mix the flour, salt, and pepper.
2. Coat each piece of chicken in the flour mixture.
3. Heat oil in a 12-inch skillet or Dutch oven over medium heat.
4. Cook the chicken in the hot oil until brown on all sides, about 15 minutes.
5. Drain excess fat from the skillet.
6. In a separate bowl, combine chopped onion, curry powder, thyme, salt, garlic, and tomatoes. Break up the tomatoes.
7. Pour the onion mixture over the chicken.
8. Heat to boiling, then reduce heat. Cover and simmer for 30 to 40 minutes or until the thickest pieces of chicken are cooked through.
9. Skim fat from the liquid if necessary.
10. Stir in currants and green bell pepper. Cover and simmer for about 5 minutes or until the bell pepper is crisp-tender.
11. Sprinkle with toasted almonds.
12. Spoon over hot cooked rice.
13. Serve with coconut and chutney if desired.
6 servings.
